what_it_does: A shared CSS substrate for product chrome and data-driven screen archetypes: phone mockups, standalone 390x844 screens, unit views, decision cards, explained presentations, composed decision views, wayfinders, and graph instruments, all styled through DNA CSS custom properties.
observations: Heavily commented with design rationale, version notes, and adversarial-critique fixes.
Defines .phone sizes and a .screen root (390x844) plus many component classes (.p-*, .ar-*, .dv-*, .ex-*, .wf-*, .gr-*).
Implements several data-driven archetypes: decision card, explained view, composed decision view, wayfinder, and graph.
Uses CSS custom properties / design tokens (--dna-*, --warm, --gold, etc.) and color-mix() for theming.
Responsive layout via viewport @media and @container queries, including landscape/desktop reflow for decision cards.
Decision options styled as tappable buttons with explicit Choose affordance, recommended state, error banner, and decided moment.
Graph instrument includes pan/zoom stage, animated dashed edges, node selection ring, and rising plain-English card.
Contains explicit depth/material treatments: recessed wells, raised plates, goldwash hatch, blueprint underlay, backdrop-filter.
Includes a two-panel comparison layout (.dv-compare-view) and an n-panel grid variant.
Has a self-documenting ledger tone: comments record intent, source comparisons, and unresolved forks.
standouts: Single CSS file unifies phone chrome, standalone screens, and multiple data-driven archetypes.
Decision-card options are rendered as tappable buttons with an explicit Choose verb and a recommended gold state.
Composed decision view (.dv-view) uses @container queries to switch between narrow stacked and wide two-column layouts.
Graph instrument (.gr-*) includes animated flowing edges, camera pan transitions, and a bottom-sheet detail card.
Inline comments function as a design ledger, tracking critiques, fixes, and source-truth decisions.
conventions: Class prefixes group by subsystem: .p-* product chrome, .ar-* archetype renderer, .dv-* decision view, .ex-* explained, .wf-* wayfinder, .gr-* graph.
Design-token-driven: spacing, type, radius, and color values reference --dna-* custom properties.
Material-depth vocabulary: nested wells, raised plates, goldwash, hatch bands, blueprint grids, backdrop-filter blur.
Responsive by scope: viewport @media for standalone, @container for contained composed views.
Signal economy: gold reserved for recommended/focal actions; bronze/ochre for secondary information.
Typography pairing: Plus Jakarta Sans for UI prose, IBM Plex Mono for technical readouts and labels.
concerns: Very large file mixing phone chrome, standalone screens, and multiple archetypes; high maintenance surface.
Extremely high selector specificity and many overrides (e.g., .dv-hold .screen.ar, .screen.ar.ar-responsive) may make future changes fragile.
Several scoped custom-property redefinitions (e.g., --nested, --goldwash inside .screen.ar) could surprise consumers if cascade order shifts.
Container-query layout relies on an ancestor with container-type, with a fallback class .dv-c that is not defined in this file.
Inline comments contain unresolved forks and flagged items (e.g., Tim's fork, flagged to projection) that are not actionable from CSS alone.
Some visual effects (backdrop-filter, color-mix, @container) have limited support in older browsers.
notes: This is the shared visual grammar for the application's product faces: a token-driven CSS ledger that styles phones, screens, and a growing family of data-driven archetypes.
questions: Where are the --dna-* custom properties and color tokens defined?
Which JavaScript renderer assigns classes like .screen.ar, .dv-view, .ex-view, .wf, .gr?
What is the host contract referenced by DECISION-CARD-HOST-CONTRACT.md?
How is the .dv-c fallback class set when container queries are unavailable?
What data sources populate the wayfinder, graph, and decision-card content?
What is the ConceptV line-icon and where are its SVG assets?
What does RHM writing cursor / rh-blink refer to in the streaming explanation state?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header says it is the shared product chrome and component tier, and the file indeed provides shared styling for phones, screens, and a broad set of app archetypes.
apparent_themes: Responsive by viewport and container scope
intention_signals: The file is trying to be a single, authoritative CSS substrate that turns data records into consistent, polished application faces, while recording the design reasoning and critique responses alongside the code.
novelty: A CSS file written partly as a design ledger, with extensive inline critique/fix annotations, container-query-based composed views, and a graph instrument with animated SVG edges and camera panning.
contribution: It contributes a comprehensive, token-driven styling vocabulary and layout system for product chrome and data-driven screen archetypes, including reusable interaction states, responsive rules, and material-depth treatments.
summary_for_embedding: A large shared CSS file that defines phone mockup chrome, standalone 390x844 screens, and data-driven archetypes such as decision cards, explained views, wayfinders, and graph instruments using DNA design tokens and warm/gold material styling.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single reusable visual substrate that renders the application's product faces and data-driven screens consistently across form factors.
